purpose: Reference for code-reviewer skill — maps each vulnerability to detection signals, language-specific patterns, and mitigation strategies

vulnerabilities.name: Broken Access Control
vulnerabilities.description: Restrictions on what authenticated users can do are not properly enforced. Attackers can access unauthorized functionality or data.
code_signals: User-supplied ID used directly to fetch records without ownership check
Role/permission check done client-side only
Missing authorization middleware on routes
IDOR: object references derived from user input with no validation
Hardcoded admin role checks that can be bypassed
Path traversal: ../../../etc/passwd in file access

python_fastapi: No role assertion after token decode
javascript_express: req.params.id used directly in DB query without checking req.user.id
java_spring: No hasRole() check before sensitive operations

vulnerabilities.mitigation: Deny by default. Enforce access control server-side on every request. Log failures. Use framework-level guards (middleware, decorators, annotations) rather than ad-hoc checks.

vulnerabilities.name: Cryptographic Failures
vulnerabilities.description: Sensitive data is transmitted or stored without adequate encryption, or uses weak/deprecated algorithms.
code_signals: Passwords stored as plain text or MD5/SHA1 hash
HTTP (not HTTPS) URLs hardcoded for API calls
Secrets or API keys committed directly in source code
Weak random number generation for security tokens (e.g., random.random() in Python)
Using ECB mode for block ciphers
SSL certificate validation disabled (verify=False)

java: new SecureRandom() seeded with predictable value

vulnerabilities.mitigation: Use bcrypt/argon2/scrypt for passwords. Use secrets module or os.urandom() for tokens. Store secrets in environment variables or a secrets manager. Always use TLS. Use AES-GCM for symmetric encryption.

vulnerabilities.name: Injection
vulnerabilities.description: Hostile data is sent to an interpreter (SQL, OS, LDAP, template engines) leading to unintended command execution.
code_signals: SQL query built with string concatenation or f-strings from user input
Shell command constructed from user-supplied values
Template rendered with unsanitized user input
LDAP filter built from user input without escaping
XML/XPath queries built with user data

javascript: db.query("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = " + req.params.id)
java: Statement.executeQuery("SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = " + id)

safe_alternatives.sql: Use parameterized queries or prepared statements: cursor.execute('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = %s', (user_id,))
safe_alternatives.shell: Avoid shell=True; pass command as list: subprocess.run(['ls', user_path])
safe_alternatives.template: Use auto-escaping template engines; never pass raw user input to render()

vulnerabilities.name: Insecure Design
vulnerabilities.description: Missing or ineffective security controls at the architecture or design level — not just implementation bugs.
code_signals: Password reset via security questions instead of secure token
Rate limiting absent on authentication endpoints
Sensitive operations (delete, transfer) with no confirmation step
Business logic that can be abused (negative quantities, price manipulation)
No separation between admin and user capabilities at the data model level
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Threat model early. Use secure design patterns (defense in depth, least privilege, fail-safe defaults). Add rate limiting, CAPTCHA, and confirmation steps for sensitive operations.

vulnerabilities.name: Security Misconfiguration
vulnerabilities.description: Insecure default configurations, incomplete setups, open cloud storage, verbose error messages, or unnecessary features enabled.
code_signals: DEBUG=True in production settings
CORS configured with wildcard origin (*) in production
Stack traces or detailed error messages returned to users
Default credentials unchanged (admin/admin, root/root)
Unnecessary HTTP methods enabled (PUT, DELETE on public endpoints)
Missing security headers (CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options)
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Automate configuration hardening. Use environment-specific config files. Implement security headers. Disable debug mode and stack traces in production. Conduct periodic configuration reviews.

vulnerabilities.name: Vulnerable and Outdated Components
vulnerabilities.description: Using components (libraries, frameworks, OS) with known vulnerabilities.
code_signals: Dependency versions pinned to old or unpinned (e.g., 'requests' without version)
No automated dependency scanning in CI/CD
requirements.txt or package.json with outdated versions
Using deprecated or known-vulnerable packages (e.g., log4j < 2.15, lodash < 4.17.21)
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Use dependency scanning tools (Dependabot, Snyk, pip-audit, npm audit). Pin dependency versions. Subscribe to CVE alerts for critical dependencies. Remove unused dependencies.

vulnerabilities.name: Identification and Authentication Failures
vulnerabilities.description: Weaknesses in authentication or session management allow attackers to compromise passwords, keys, or session tokens.
code_signals: No rate limiting or lockout on login endpoint
Weak password policy (no minimum length, no complexity)
Session tokens with insufficient entropy (too short, predictable)
Session not invalidated on logout
JWT with algorithm=none accepted
Hardcoded credentials in code or config files
Passwords compared with == instead of constant-time comparison

python: if password == stored_password:  # timing attack risk
javascript: req.session.userId = req.body.userId  # trusting user input for session

vulnerabilities.mitigation: Implement MFA. Use adaptive rate limiting and account lockout. Validate JWT algorithm explicitly. Invalidate sessions on logout. Use secure, HttpOnly, SameSite cookies. Use constant-time comparison for secrets.

vulnerabilities.name: Software and Data Integrity Failures
vulnerabilities.description: Code and infrastructure that does not protect against integrity violations — including insecure deserialization and CI/CD pipeline attacks.
code_signals: pickle.loads() used on untrusted data
YAML.load() (not safe_load) on user-supplied YAML
JSON deserialization with auto-instantiation of arbitrary classes
npm packages installed from unverified sources
No integrity checks (checksums) on downloaded files or plugins
CI/CD pipeline with no secret scanning or SAST

javascript: JSON.parse with reviver that instantiates classes

vulnerabilities.mitigation: Never deserialize untrusted data with pickle, marshal, or similar. Use yaml.safe_load(). Implement digital signatures for critical updates. Use SRI for CDN assets. Add SAST and secret scanning to CI/CD.

vulnerabilities.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
vulnerabilities.description: Insufficient logging, monitoring, or alerting prevents timely detection and response to breaches.
code_signals: No logging on authentication events (login success/failure)
No logging on sensitive operations (data export, privilege escalation, deletion)
Logs contain sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII)
Exception handlers that swallow errors silently (bare except: pass)
No correlation IDs for request tracing
Log injection: user input included in log messages without sanitization

python: logging.info(f'User {password} logged in')  # logging sensitive data

vulnerabilities.mitigation: Log all auth events, privilege changes, and sensitive operations. Use structured logging (JSON). Never log credentials or PII. Set up alerting on anomalous patterns. Store logs in tamper-evident storage.

vulnerabilities.name: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF)
vulnerabilities.description: The application fetches a remote resource using a URL supplied or influenced by the user, enabling internal network access.
code_signals: URL passed directly from user input to requests.get(), fetch(), urllib
Webhook or callback URL not validated against an allowlist
File path or URL in API request not sanitized
Redirect URLs not validated (open redirect leading to SSRF)
Cloud metadata endpoint accessible: http://169.254.169.254/

python: httpx.get(url)  # where url is from user input

vulnerabilities.mitigation: Validate and sanitize all user-supplied URLs. Use an allowlist of permitted domains. Block requests to private IP ranges (10.x, 172.16.x, 192.168.x, 169.254.x). Disable HTTP redirects or validate each redirect destination.
